title: Frederic.
content:
(Time Night. Scene the woods.)


В В В В Where shall I turn me? whither shall I bend
В В В В My weary way? thus worn with toil and faint
В В В В How thro' the thorny mazes of this wood
В В В В Attain my distant dwelling? that deep cry
В В В В That rings along the forest seems to sound
В В В В My parting knell: it is the midnight howl
В В В В Of hungry monsters prowling for their prey!
В В В В Again! oh save me--save me gracious Heaven!
В В В В I am not fit to die!
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В  Thou coward wretch
В В В В Why heaves thy trembling heart? why shake thy limbs
В В В В Beneath their palsied burden? is there ought
В В В В So lovely in existence? would'st thou drain
В В В В Even to its dregs the bitter draught of life?
В В В В Dash down the loathly bowl! poor outcast slave
В В В В Stamp'd with the brand of Vice and Infamy
В В В В Why should the villain Frederic shrink from Death?

В В В В Death! where the magic in that empty name
В В В В That chills my inmost heart? why at the thought
В В В В Starts the cold dew of fear on every limb?
В В В В There are no terrors to surround the Grave,
В В В В When the calm Mind collected in itself
В В В В Surveys that narrow house: the ghastly train
В В В В That haunt the midnight of delirious Guilt
В В В В Then vanish; in that home of endless rest
В В В В All sorrows cease.--Would I might slumber there!

В В В В Why then this panting of the fearful heart?
В В В В This miser love of Life that dreads to lose
В В В В Its cherish'd torment? shall the diseased man
В В В В Yield up his members to the surgeon's knife,
В В В В Doubtful of succour, but to ease his frame
В В В В Of fleshly anguish, and the coward wretch,
В В В В Whose ulcered soul can know no human help
В В В В Shrink from the best Physician's certain aid?
В В В В Oh it were better far to lay me down
В В В В Here on this cold damp earth, till some wild beast
В В В В Seize on his willing victim!

В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В If to die
В В В В Were all, it were most sweet to rest my head
В В В В On the cold clod, and sleep the sleep of Death.
В В В В But if the Archangel's trump at the last hour
В В В В Startle the ear of Death and wake the soul
В В В В To frenzy!--dreams of infancy! fit tales
В В В В For garrulous beldames to affrighten babes!
В В В В I have been guilty, yet my mind can bear
В В В В The retrospect of guilt, yet in the hour
В В В В Of deep contrition to THE ETERNAL look
В В В В For mercy! for the child of Poverty,
В В В В And "disinherited of happiness,"

В В В В What if I warr'd upon the world? the world
В В В В Had wrong'd me first: I had endur'd the ills
В В В В Of hard injustice; all this goodly earth
В В В В Was but to me one wild waste wilderness;
В В В В I had no share in Nature's patrimony,
В В В В Blasted were all my morning hopes of Youth,
В В В В Dark DISAPPOINTMENT follow'd on my ways,
В В В В CARE was my bosom inmate, and keen WANT
В В В В Gnaw'd at my heart. ETERNAL ONE thou know'st
В В В В How that poor heart even in the bitter hour
В В В В Of lewdest revelry has inly yearn'd
В В В В For peace!

В В В В В В В В В В В В  My FATHER! I will call on thee,
В В В В Pour to thy mercy seat my earnest prayer,
В В В В And wait thy peace in bowedness of soul.
В В В В Oh thoughts of comfort! how the afflicted heart,
В В В В Tired with the tempest of its passions, rests
В В В В On you with holy hope! the hollow howl
В В В В Of yonder harmless tenant of the woods
В В В В Bursts not with terror on the sober'd sense.
В В В В If I have sinn'd against mankind, on them
В В В В Be that past sin; they made me what I was.
В В В В In these extremest climes can Want no more
В В В В Urge to the deeds of darkness, and at length
В В В В Here shall I rest. What tho' my hut be poor--
В В В В The rains descend not thro' its humble roof:
В В В В Would I were there again! the night is cold;
В В В В And what if in my wanderings I should rouse
В В В В The savage from his thicket!

В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В  Hark! the gun!
В В В В And lo--the fire of safety! I shall reach
В В В В My little hut again! again by toil
В В В В Force from the stubborn earth my sustenance,
В В В В And quick-ear'd guilt will never start alarm'd
В В В В Amid the well-earn'd meal. This felon's garb--
В В В В Will it not shield me from the winds of Heaven?
В В В В And what could purple more? Oh strengthen me
В В В В Eternal One in this serener state!
В В В В Cleanse thou mine heart, so PENITENCE and FAITH
В В В В Shall heal my soul and my last days be peace.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Southey, titled "Frederic.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...
